goal: Licensing and entitlement model for a global OEM (850 potential users, 120 concurrent peak, 11 countries).
thoughts: Pure advisory. Sparse concurrent usage (120/850) = concurrent licenses are the obvious economic fit if available. Stay grounded in real Omniverse Enterprise structure — do NOT invent tiers. Offline grace: Enterprise licenses typically support a finite disconnect window via cached entitlement — exact duration is product-specific, have Alessandro ask NVIDIA. License-server topology: primary in central data center, standby in secondary region, clients fail over automatically. Don't quote $/seat. Prepare procurement talking points.
code:
# Advisory — no scene mutation. Structure the licensing conversation.
# 1. Ground in real NVIDIA product structure
lookup_product_spec(product="Omniverse Enterprise")
lookup_product_spec(product="Isaac Sim entitlement")
lookup_knowledge(query="omniverse enterprise concurrent named license model offline grace period")
lookup_knowledge(query="nvidia ngc enterprise license server topology failover")

# 2. Sizing for 850 users / 120 concurrent peak
#   - If concurrent is available: size to 120 + headroom (e.g. 150).
#   - If named-only: 850 — much larger ask, argue concurrent with NVIDIA commercial.

# 3. Topology: primary + standby license server
#   - Primary: central data center (align with Nucleus home)
#   - Standby: secondary region
#   - Clients configured with both; automatic failover on primary unreachable.

# 4. Offline/disconnected use
#   - Enterprise licenses support cached entitlement for a finite window.
#   - Exact duration depends on edition — verify with NVIDIA commercial.
#   - Work-in-progress: running simulations do NOT terminate mid-run if server
#     becomes unreachable — entitlement is checked at startup and on refresh.

# 5. Procurement talking points Alessandro can paste
# Ask NVIDIA:
#   (a) concurrent vs named for Isaac Sim specifically in Omniverse Enterprise
#   (b) offline grace window, measured in hours/days
#   (c) failover behavior when primary license server is down
#   (d) license-server placement requirements for 11-country footprint
#   (e) multi-region entitlement — one pool or per-region pools
# Do NOT commit to a $/seat figure from memory.
failure_modes: assistant invents license tiers that don't exist
assistant claims running simulations die on license-server unreachable (they don't)
assistant promises a specific offline-grace duration without caveats
